evals.prompt: I want to build a CLI tool in Node.js that wraps our company's REST API for managing projects. The API uses API key auth and has endpoints for listing, creating, updating, and deleting projects. I want it to be usable by coding agents like Claude Code.
evals.expected_output: A Node.js CLI project with Commander.js, buildXxxCall pure functions, structured JSON output with --json flag, error hints with What+Why+Hint format, and unit tests for build functions
expectations: Project uses Commander.js or similar CLI framework
Build functions are pure (no I/O, no side effects)
Output supports --json flag for structured JSON
Errors follow What+Why+Hint format
Unit tests cover build function argument mapping
Commands follow Search-Fetch-Act pattern (list/get/create/update/delete)
TTY detection is implemented for output formatting
An escape hatch command (api) exists for direct API access

evals.prompt: Create a Python CLI using Typer that wraps a Notion-like API with OAuth authentication. It should support search, page CRUD, and database queries. Coding agents will be the primary users.
evals.expected_output: A Python CLI with Typer, OAuth 2.0 PKCE auth flow, structured output, error hint system, and comprehensive help text
expectations: Project uses Typer or Click
OAuth 2.0 with PKCE authentication is implemented
Token storage uses OS-specific config directories with proper permissions (0o600)
All commands support --json and --raw output modes
Error messages include What, Why, and Hint components
Help text exists at root, subcommand, and flag levels
Pure functions separate CLI args from API call construction

evals.prompt: I need a Go CLI tool using Cobra that talks to our internal gRPC service. It has about 40 endpoints grouped into users, projects, billing, and analytics. The main users are AI coding assistants in our CI pipeline. How should I structure this?
evals.expected_output: Architecture recommendation with Go/Cobra scaffold using search+execute pattern for the large API surface, structured output, and the four agent-first principles
expectations: Recommends subcommand groups for the 4 domains
Includes an api escape hatch command for less-used endpoints
Uses Cobra framework
Output defaults to JSON for agent consumption
Error handling follows structured What+Why+Hint format
Suggests promoting most-used endpoints to dedicated commands
Testing strategy focuses on pure function unit tests

evals.prompt: Help me add proper error handling to my existing CLI tool. Right now errors just print a stack trace and agents keep retrying the same failing command. I'm using Rust with Clap. The tool wraps a REST API.
evals.expected_output: Structured error handling implementation with What+Why+Hint format, pattern-matching hint rules, JSON error output, and Rust/Clap-specific guidance
expectations: Errors restructured into What+Why+Hint format
Hint rules use pattern matching (regex) against error messages
JSON error format works with --json flag
Hints provide actionable next steps that agents can follow
Implementation uses Rust error handling idioms (thiserror or anyhow)
Common patterns covered: not-found, unauthorized, rate-limited, invalid-input
